slides.title: Understanding Decision Trees
slides.subtitle: A Guide to Decision Trees in Machine Learning

slides.title: Introduction to Decision Trees
slides.content: Decision trees are a nonlinear model used for classification and regression tasks. They mimic human decision-making processes by breaking down data into smaller subsets while at the same time developing an associated decision tree incrementally.

slides.title: Structure of Decision Trees
slides.content: A decision tree is composed of nodes representing decisions, branches representing outcomes, and leaf nodes representing final decisions or classifications.

slides.title: Example: Building an Ad Blocker
slides.content: Decision trees can be used to classify images on a webpage as banner advertisements or page content, demonstrating their applicability in real-world scenarios.

slides.title: Advantages of Decision Trees
slides.content: They are simple to understand and interpret, require little data preparation, and can handle both numerical and categorical data.

slides.title: Limitations of Decision Trees
slides.content: Decision trees can be prone to overfitting, especially with complex trees. They can be unstable with small variations in data leading to a different tree being generated.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: While decision trees are not frequently used independently in practice, they are crucial components of more sophisticated models like random forests and boosting algorithms, making their understanding essential.
